name: AwardPing global monitoring policy
principles: Publish updates only when a concrete applicant-facing award fact changed.
Apply policy globally across all awards unless a rule explicitly says it is award-specific.
Prefer deterministic local rejection before using AI, and use the same policy flags when AI output is post-filtered.
Keep questionable source-quality cleanup conservative by moving bad sources to review_later unless a task explicitly asks for deletion.
true_update_categories: application, nomination, recommendation, transcript, essay, interview, or submission requirement changes
award amount, stipend, tuition, travel, or funding changes
document/PDF/guideline wording changes when the changed applicant-facing text can be named
official source page additions or removals that affect how applicants or advisors use the award

policy_flags.label: Relative recency label churn
policy_flags.prompt: Reject relative recency label churn on news/listing items, such as '8 days ago' becoming '9 days ago', unless a concrete applicant-facing award fact also changed.
examples: A recent news item changes from '8 days ago' to '9 days ago'.
A latest-updates card changes from 'yesterday' to '2 days ago'.

policy_flags.label: Current-date or last-updated-only churn
policy_flags.prompt: Reject current-date-only, generated timestamp, last-updated-only, updated-date, post-id, writer-id, and view-count churn unless a concrete applicant-facing award fact also changed.

policy_flags.label: Recipient, finalist, alumni, or press churn
policy_flags.prompt: Reject news, press, alumni-highlight, shared-from, recipient, finalist, cohort, student profile, and awardee churn unless it changes applicant-facing award rules.

policy_flags.label: Profile, testimonial, roster, or carousel rotation
policy_flags.prompt: Reject rotating testimonials, featured fellows, alumni/profile rosters, speaker bios, staff/team rosters, and profile/story text unless application requirements, deadlines, eligibility, or funding changed.

policy_flags.label: Document metadata or file-only change
policy_flags.prompt: Reject PDF, Word, downloadable-file hash, byte-size, and metadata-only changes unless the actual changed wording, date, requirement, amount, or applicant instruction is visible and can be named.

policy_flags.label: Donation, fundraising, checkout, or sponsorship widget
policy_flags.prompt: Reject donation, fundraising, checkout/cart, gift amount, donor, sponsor, and sponsorship widget changes unless applicant-facing award information changed.

policy_flags.label: Navigation, sidebar, FAQ order, or layout-only change
policy_flags.prompt: Reject navigation, footer, sidebar, social/share widget, FAQ order, link order, content reordering, font, reflow, lazy-image, and layout-only changes unless a concrete award fact changed.

policy_flags.label: Calendar, conference, admissions-event, or generic event churn
policy_flags.prompt: Reject calendar, conference, admissions-event, webinar, workshop, event registration, and news/event listing churn unless award deadlines, applications, eligibility, funding, or requirements changed.

policy_flags.label: Transient site chrome or notice
policy_flags.prompt: Reject cookie banners, consent popups, ads, newsletter popups, CAPTCHA/math questions, sitewide notices, holiday or operating-hours banners, countdown timers, access notices, and unrelated page widgets unless award requirements changed.

policy_flags.label: Animated statistic or impact-number counter
policy_flags.prompt: Reject animated or count-up statistic counters, KPI widgets, impact-number widgets, and loading-state number drift unless an award amount or applicant-facing funding fact changed.

policy_flags.label: Access, security, or error page
policy_flags.prompt: Reject access denied, forbidden, security challenge, not found, source error, and source access recovery pages as non-updates.

policy_flags.label: Raw scrape artifact
policy_flags.prompt: Reject raw scrape artifacts, leaked markup, jump-link prefixes, menu blobs, CTAs, and vague fragments such as LEARN MORE.

policy_flags.label: Generic latest-updates block
policy_flags.prompt: Reject generic Latest Updates blocks and cross-program latest-news sidebars unless the changed item is the monitored award's applicant-facing information.

source_quality_stipulations.label: Generic search, tag, category, listing, or open-data facet pages should not remain monitored sources.
Sibling program, broad agency, archive, policy, unrelated research/news, job, staff, alumni, recipient, or profile pages should not bloat an award outline.
Privacy, terms, cookie, login, donation, cart, annual report, bylaws, media kit, and sponsorship pages are not award-monitoring sources.
Permanent errors, soft 404s, gated private documents, blocked third-party pages, and oversized files should be classified consistently before they produce updates.
